prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Reference DOM (Checkbox group no fieldset): Checkbox options as bare label>input pairs without fieldset or legend Reference DOM (Other option reveal): Referral source Other checkbox reveals free-text input Reference DOM (Fields inside details accordion): Cover letter and visa fields hidden inside closed details element Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: pill-yes-no, aria-labelledby-hidden, label-wraps-deep, shadow-host, details-accordion. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Checkbox group no fieldset; Other option reveal; Fields inside details accordion.
